pages.reason: TOC and callouts stay readable, but macro output adds extra warning blocks and unresolved noise to an already dense hub page.
Status collapses to plain shouted text while the warning and task list remain understandable enough for AI extraction.
The expand macro becomes a clean details block and preserves the leaf-page narrative without major structural noise.
Code and noformat blocks stay sharply delimited, so the page remains one of the clearest macro-heavy Markdown outputs.
Page Properties turns into front-matter-like text and Page Properties Report degrades into a thin placeholder table, so meaning survives but shape is lossy.
Navigation macros dump mixed headings, expand/collapse chrome, duplicated excerpt content, and one raw pageId URL, which is stable but poor for AI comprehension.
Gallery and attachments macros materialize as bulky Markdown tables that are readable but noisy and repetitive.
Status collapses to bare uppercase text and the image attachment turns into an unknown-attachment placeholder, so semantic loss is obvious even though export stays stable.
The expand block is fine, but the children macro injects extra heading/list structure that muddies the deep-branch narrative.
The excerpt lands as a plain readable paragraph, so the page stays coherent despite the surrounding clutter.
Both callouts map cleanly to admonition-style Markdown and keep the leaf-page warnings explicit.
The info macro becomes a short admonition and does not materially damage this otherwise simple sibling page.
The note macro is compact and readable, and the rest of the page structure stays conventional Markdown.
The tip remains readable, but attachment references degrade to hash links and the page carries heavy unresolved-title noise.
